forensic_packet.export_safe_summary: M5 forensic packets remain metadata-safe and redaction-aware; locality and egress state stay explicit across local-only, imported, mirrored, and uploaded artifacts.

drills.summary: Notebook kernel stall preserves the last cell-run checkpoint, narrows only the kernel lane, forbids hidden rerun, and keeps crash/support export local-first.
Provider-run failure revokes authority, scopes failure to the provider lane, preserves ticket metadata, and requires explicit reviewed upload for any shared packet.
Preview-server restart exhausts only the dev-server budget, preserves route metadata, and keeps mirrored-symbol lookup explicit.
Remote connector drift narrows only the connector lane, keeps request metadata checkpointed, and preserves imported-vs-live truth in export.
AI broker breaker-open drills prove scoped failure, explicit replay boundaries, and no silent export broadening when provider egress narrows.
Query/runtime crash isolates to the request lane, preserves reviewed request metadata, and blocks hidden rerun behind an explicit restart path.
Pipeline-viewer fault keeps imported event lineage explicit and proves reconnect loops do not silently rerun or widen export scope.
Connector host mismatch forces visible fail-closed state, preserves target metadata, and keeps upload review explicit.
Docs/browser bridge drift keeps imported route facts explicit and preserves a local-only handoff path.
Profiler/replay imported-gap drill keeps partial mapping quality visible and export-safe without forced upload.
Infrastructure helper signature failure proves fail-closed restart policy, cache-snapshot checkpoint preservation, and explicit mirror review.

export_safe_summary: Seeded M5 host-failure drills cover restart-budget enforcement, scoped failure, checkpoint preservation, export-safe forensics, and no-silent-upload rules across every claimed host family.
